description_long: This traffic barricade consists of a sturdy A-frame structure with two wide, rectangular panels mounted horizontally, each featuring alternating orange and white diagonal stripes. The panels are attached to a metallic or composite frame, which is supported by four angled legs that provide stability. The surface of the panels shows signs of wear, with visible scuff marks and some dirt, indicating outdoor use. The frame appears to be made of metal or heavy-duty plastic, with bolts or rivets securing the panels in place. There are no visible lights or electronic components, and the barricade is designed to be portable and easily repositioned. The overall construction is robust, suitable for temporary road closures or construction zones in urban environments.
description: A robust A-frame traffic barricade with two orange and white striped panels, supported by four angled legs and a metallic or composite frame. The panels show signs of outdoor use and are securely fastened for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing both orange and white striped panels, with the frame and support legs visible behind.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the A-frame structure and the angled positioning of the support legs, with minimal panel visibility.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped panels and frame, but with some structural details more visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the A-frame legs and the depth of the barricade, with little panel detail.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, alternating, each stripe approximately equal width, repeated across both panels.
